question_text: Explain what the law of conservation of energy states. Give an example using a swinging pendulum.
correct_answer: The law of conservation of energy states that energy cannot be created or destroyed, only transferred from one form to another. In a pendulum: at the highest point, all energy is gravitational potential energy (GPE); as it swings down, GPE converts to kinetic energy (KE); at the lowest point, all energy is kinetic energy; as it swings up the other side, KE converts back to GPE. The total mechanical energy remains constant (ignoring friction and air resistance).
explanation: This is a fundamental physics principle. Energy changes form but the total amount stays the same. A pendulum is an excellent example because you can clearly see the energy transformation between gravitational potential energy and kinetic energy as it swings back and forth.
